entries.rationale: Brian's resonant, warm, trustworthy baritone with an older American voice sits back in the chest and carries the weighted, unhurried cadence Kowalski demands — resonant enough to fill silence without projecting, and seasoned enough to feel like a man who has waited six years for this conversation. Stability is pushed high to reflect his iron control; style is kept low to preserve the flat investigative affect, and speed is slowed to honor the deliberate, pause-heavy delivery.

Sarah's soft, calm, professional young-adult American voice provides the intimate close-mic quality Maya requires — a quiet dynamic baseline that can carry suppressed grief and micro-tremors without tipping into theatrical crying or confident narrator brightness. Stability is kept moderate to allow the fragile composure to crack at edges; style is nudged up slightly to carry the emotional undercurrent of grief actively held in check.

Matilda's friendly, natural, warm American adult voice approximates the lived-in diner-familiar ease Rosie needs — unhurried, approachable, with enough natural texture to suggest a woman who has poured a million cups without competing with the scene's tension. Stability is kept high for composure and low arousal; style is moderate to allow the gentle melodic lilt at line endings without theatricality.
